assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies Service A as 'HIGH RISK - Will Block Upgrades' and explains it requires ALL pods to remain available with no pods able to be evicted

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Response identifies Service B as 'HIGH RISK - Will Block Upgrades' and states it's 'Identical to 100% available - no pods can be evicted'

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Response marks Service C as 'SAFE - Upgrade Compatible' and explains it 'Allows 1 pod to be evicted (3 total - 2 required = 1 available for eviction)'

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Response marks Service D as 'SAFE - Upgrade Compatible' and explains it 'Explicitly allows 1 pod to be unavailable at a time'

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific YAML example changing Service A to minAvailable: 80% and includes kubectl patch commands with alternative configurations

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl patch command for Service B with 'maxUnavailable':'20%' which achieves the same effect as maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during node drain' and 'After 1 hour, GKE force-evicts pods regardless of PDB'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl patch commands for both Service A and Service B PDBs with proper syntax and values

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Response does not mention testing changes in non-production environments before applying to production
